question_text: A student builds an electromagnet with a coil of 50 turns. She then rebuilds it with the same coil but now has 100 turns. If the electromagnet could previously pick up 8 metal paperclips, approximately how many paperclips could it pick up with 100 turns (assuming the same current)?
explanation: Doubling the number of turns in an electromagnet doubles the strength of its magnetic field. Since the strength doubles, the electromagnet can pick up approximately twice as many paperclips (8 × 2 = 16).
